page_content:
# Dr. jafari  
* the lab PI/Doctor overseeing the lab had very little contact with me, though he was my initial point of contact
* the Phd student that did supervise me moved to Italy for his postdoc
* Dr Jafari supervising me taught an elective that I did not take
* had a few interactions in the lab were we would do literature reviews and talk about some of the research papers
* had lab open houses were people would come in and had military inspectors come in when he was applying for grant money
* he also talked with me about my senior design product and viewed how clear the silicon aortic valve model was, we were comparing our project with he vendors project we were replacing. I distinctly remember comparing the clarity with him ( because the vendor product was clear but opaque) whereas our product was completely clear when submerged in water which was important for our PID laser system. THe laser was extremely powerful and any opaqueness/cloudiness would result in the laser diffracting and people potentially going blind as well as inaccuracies introduced during the actual simulation with particles to measure hemodynamic accuracy
